LEXINGTON CITY FIRE CHIEF REPORTED THAT FROM JACKTOWN ROAD INTO THE SOUTHERN PART OF LEXINGTON CITY, MULTIPLE LARGE TREES WERE EITHER TOPPED OUT OR COMPLETELY DOWNED. APPROXIMATELY 600 CUSTOMERS LOST ELECTRICITY WITH DOMINION ENERGY AS THE RESULT OF A LARGE TREE TAKING OUT ELECTRICAL SERVICE LINES AND TWO UTILITY POLES WITHIN THE CITY LIMITS. ANOTHER STRUCTURE WAS DAMAGED BY A LARGE DOWNED PINE TREE. THE FAMILY WAS REQUIRED TO RELOCATE. THERE WERE ALSO A COUPLE OF OTHER TREES DOWN ON STRUCTURES WITH MINOR DAMAGE REPORTED. A LOT OF DEBRIS CONTINUES TO SCATTER THE ROADWAYS.
